folio.label: 172v
folio.l: nat ouer hastifly ,

for a man æ is to desiryngö to gete richesses
aboundenetú hi~ first to thefte , & to alle othere yueles

¢ And æä-
fore seitú Salamou~ ; he æ hastetú hi~ to bisily to wexe riche , shal
be noon Innocent

¢ He Seitú also ; æ the richesse æ hastily comeæ
to a man , sone & ligútly passetú & gootú from a man ,

But æat
richesse æ cometú litel & litel , wexetú alwey & multiplietú ,,

¢ And Sire ©e shullen gete richesses by ©ourö wyt & by ©oure t-
uaille vn to ©oure ¸fit ,

& that w outen wrongö , or harm
doynge to any other ¹sone ,

for the lawe seitú , there maketú
no man hi~ self riche , if he do harm to a nother wigút ,

this is
to seyn ; that nature defendetú & forbedetú by rigút , æ no
man make hi~ self riche , vn to the harm of a nother ¹sone ,

And Tullius seitú ; æ no sorwe , ne no drede of detú , ne no
thyngö æ may falle vn to a man ,

is so muchel a geyns nature
as a man to encreese his owne ¸fit , to the harm of a nother man

And thougú the grete men & the mygúty men geten richesses
moore ligútly , than æè ,

©et shalt æè nat be Idel ne slow , to do æé
¸fit , for æè shalt in alle wyse , flee ydelnesse

¢ For Salamon
seitú ; æ ydelnesse otiose dot on y/ODR/ techetú a man , to do manye yueles

¢ And
the same Salamon seitú ; æ he that tuailletú & bisietú hi~
to tilien his land , shal ete breed ,

But he æ is ydel , & castetú
hi~ to no bysinesse ne ocupaco~n , shal falle in to pouertee and
dye for hunger ,

And he æ is ydel and slow , kan neuäe fynde co-
uenable tyme for to do his ¸fit

¢ For æeise cau-
ses seitú Catou~ ; waketú , & enclynetú ©ow nat , ouä muchel to
Slepe , for ouä muchel reste , norisshetú & causetú manye vices

¢ And æäfore seitú Seynt Ierome ; Dootú so~me goode dedes
æ the deuele which is ©oure enemy , ne fynde ©ow nat , vn-
ocupied ,

for the deuele ne taketú nat ligútly vn to his wer-
kynge , swicú as he fynt ocupied in goode werkes

¢ Tha~ne
thus , In getyngö richesses , ©e musten flee ydelnesse otiose dot on y/ODR/ ,

And aft ä-
ward , ©e shuln vsen the richesses , whicú ©e han geten , by ©ourö
wit , & by ©oure tuaille

For rigút as men blamen , an auaricious
man by cause of his scarsitee & chyncherie ,

in the same wy-
se is he to blame , æ spendetú ouä largely

¢ And æäfore seitú Ca-
tou~ ; vse he seitú thy richesse , æ æè hast I geten

in swicú a ma-
nere , æ men haue no matere ne cause to calle the , neiæä wrec-
che ne chynche ,

for it is a gret shame to a man , to haue a
poore herte & a riche purs ,

he seitú also , æe goodes æ æè hast
I geten , vse he~ by mesure , that is to seyn ; spende mesurably
